item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Less is more, sometimes. I like this for being a geological oddity with no handy note included to explain it`s reason for being.

That the island may have a use to unknown visitors who like stealing invasive equipment is a subtle punch.

There are so many SCP`s, and often they reach for apocalyptic greatness or parody some other SCP, or pander to tiresome storylines. It`s not always bad but sometimes it seems needy or grasping. SCP 3991 has cool time haxx +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will agree this does nothing new, but its certainly not bad, just very bland. Novote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how to wrap my head around the time dilation thing. Does that basically mean that in the time it takes the team to move in, do what they need to, and leave, from the outside it looks like they've been in there for a tiny fraction of a second, while from their own perspective it looks like the rest of the world is slowing down to almost a complete standstill?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will agree with your friend, this is a good place for writing papers.

The writing is solid, and although like what LordMetalton mentioned, it's a bit bland, it's enough to secure a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe that wasn't the right word, but what I meant by that is it took a basic concept and didn't really do anything interesting with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is competent, but the actual item is too straightforward. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a nod to my friend, who is a CS student and mentioned that the island would be useful to type papers on. I can get rid of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This started of being potentially interesting, but didn't go anywhere. It's just your bog standard time dilation effect, and the interview and experiment logs add nothing that wasn't already stated earlier in the article.
Also the entry with the college student visitor is massively immersion breaking. You realise that the Foundation is a highly secretive organisation, right? They don't have random student visitors.

item.title: Picture Credits
item.content:encoded:
Picture obtained from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ball%27s_Pyramid_(37050227970).jpg

Not sure if I did this right, please advise me if anything needs to be changed with the credits.
